name: Embalse de Arguis – circular route
region_intro: Hiking around Arguis offers access to diverse natural features near Huesca, characterized by its proximity to the Gratal and El Águila mountains. The region features the 18th-century Arguis Reservoir and serves as a gateway to the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, known for its rocky cliffs and limestone canyons. The landscape includes traditional Pyrenean-style villages and significant geological formations. Best hiking trails around Arguis The most popular hiking route is Embalse de Arguis – circular route, an easy 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete, circling the central reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tozal de Gratal – circular from Arguis, a difficult 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This route involves significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Local hikers also love the Arguis Reservoir – Arguis Reservoir loop from Arguis, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes around the reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Arguis is defined by the Arguis Reservoir, limestone formations, and mountainous terrain, with routes offering options for different ability levels from easy strolls to challenging ascents. The routes in Arguis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Arguis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Arguis?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes available around Arguis, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy strolls, moderate paths, and more challenging ascents through mountainous terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes around Arguis?
A: Hiking around Arguis offers diverse natural features. The central highlight is the 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which many routes circle. You can also explore the dramatic limestone formations and gorges, such as the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgas de San Julián&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique geological features. The area also provides views of prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de Gratal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Arguis?
A: Yes, Arguis offers easy paths suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947219993/embalse-de-arguis-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.4-mile (5.4 km) trail that circles the reservoir, perfect for a leisurely walk with children.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Arguis?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Arguis region, especially on public footpaths. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, particularly in protected areas like the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Arguis?
A: Yes, many routes in Arguis are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947219993/embalse-de-arguis-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947090869/tozal-de-gratal-circular-from-arguis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de Gratal – circular from Arguis&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arguis?
A: The best time to hike in Arguis is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher trails might be affected by snow or ice, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Arguis?
A: Parking is generally available in the village of Arguis itself, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. There are also designated parking areas near the Arguis Reservoir and at various trailheads, making access convenient for hikers.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Arguis?
A: While Arguis is a small village, public transport options might be limited. It's generally recommended to travel by car to access the various trailheads around the reservoir and into the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park for maximum flexibility.

Q: Do I need any special permits to hike in the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park?
A: For general hiking on marked trails within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, permits are typically not required. However, if you plan to engage in specific activities like canyoning or certain climbing routes, permits may be necessary. It's always best to check with the Arguis Interpretation Center for the latest regulations.

Q: Are there any amenities or places to eat near the trails in Arguis?
A: The picturesque village of Arguis itself offers some amenities, including places to eat. It's a charming spot with traditional stone houses, making it a pleasant place to start or finish your hike and find refreshments.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Arguis?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the serene reservoir to the dramatic mountainous terrain, and the well-maintained trails that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls on the trails near Arguis?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgas de San Julián&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument where you can witness water emerging from the gorge, with some sections featuring water falling from the mountaintop, creating a beautiful and unique sight.
tips: This picturesque town is reminiscent of the Pyrenean villages for its stone houses topped by the typical scarecrows on their chimneys. It also has a beautiful Romanesque-style church dating from the 12th century.

This is the oldest reservoir in Aragon. It was under construction for 24 years and was completed in 1704. It dams the seasonal waters of the Isuela River and is used exclusively for irrigation (not counting, in addition, fishing), mainly from the Hoya de Huesca.
